headline: US strikes reach tenth night on Iran
deck: American forces pressed strikes into a tenth successive night with no announced halt. Houthi warnings turned two Saudi crude tankers in the Red Sea while Tehran denied talks and ASEAN ministers registered serious concern.
body: American forces completed the latest package of strikes on Iranian targets on 21 July, extending the campaign through a tenth successive night. A reported 10-day ceasefire proposal that had circulated the previous day produced no official pause in operations. Iran’s embassy in Seoul issued a statement denying any negotiations with Washington. The public record shows continued action rather than a verified halt. [E1][E3]

US Central Command issued its own release on the most recent precision strikes against Iranian military targets. The successive nights form an unbroken sequence of documented military activity. Official language from the command describes the completion of the latest package. The continuity of the campaign rests on these successive accounts. [E2][E1]

Iran’s diplomatic mission in Seoul formally rejected claims of ceasefire negotiations with the United States. Tehran maintains its stated posture of control over the Strait of Hormuz. No Iranian statement places an off-ramp on the record. The denial leaves the diplomatic channel closed in public view. [E3]

Houthi warnings to shipping companies to avoid Saudi ports prompted two Saudi crude tankers to turn back in the Red Sea. The episode opens a second maritime front at the Bab el-Mandeb strait. Pressure on commercial traffic now registers on both the northern and southern approaches. Geographic expansion of the risk accompanies the ongoing air campaign. [E4]

UK Maritime Trade Operations rates the threat level in the Strait of Hormuz as severe. The rating coincides with the new warnings affecting Red Sea routes. Simultaneous strain on two primary chokepoints multiplies the constraints facing energy shipping. The conflict’s reach now spans Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb together. [E5][E4]

ASEAN’s top diplomats convened under the shadow of the US-Iran conflict and voiced serious concern. The 10-day ceasefire proposal remains a reported idea rather than a confirmed agreement. No government has announced a formal pause. Diplomatic expressions of unease have not yet produced a mediating track. [E6]

The campaign compounds across two seas while strikes enter their tenth night. Houthi actions add a southern front to the pressure already registered at Hormuz. Tehran continues to deny talks and holds its stated position. The only ceasefire anyone can point to remains one no government has signed. [E1][E4][E3]

evidence_box.fragment: US finishes latest strikes against Iran

evidence_box.source: Embassy of the Islamic Republic of Iran (Seoul)
evidence_box.fragment: denying ceasefire negotiations with the United States
Houthis warn shipping companies avoid Saudi ports

art.caption: A strike campaign enters its tenth night as the danger widens from one strait to two; the only ceasefire anyone can point to is one no government has signed.
